zh.Tichborne, Hampshire, England, United Ki: 英國英格蘭哈普爾迪賀波內
英國英格蘭哈普爾迪賀波內
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon於1480年出生在英國英格蘭哈普爾迪賀波內時，他的父親John Tichbourne III, Esq.20歲，母親Margaret Martin20歲。他與Anne White結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1535年在他的家鄉享年55歲，埋葬在英國哈普爾迪賀波內。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon于1480年出生在英国英格兰哈普爾迪賀波內时，他的父亲John Tichbourne III, Esq.20岁，母亲Margaret Martin20岁。他与Anne White结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1535年在他的家乡享年55岁，埋葬在英国哈普爾迪賀波內。

nameMeanings.name: Tichborne,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Tichborne (Hants).

variants.name: Tichband
meanings: English: variant of Tichborne with excrescent -d. Compare Chadband , Goodband , Osband , and Rathband .

variants.name: Tichbon
meanings: English:  see  Tichborne  .

variants.name: Tichbourne
meanings: English:  see  Tichborne  .

nameMeanings.name: Sheriff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name or nickname from Middle English shirreve, sher(r)eve, sheref(fe) ‘sheriff, officer of the Crown’ (Old English scīrgerēfa, from scīr ‘shire, county’ + (ge)rēfa ‘reeve’), literally the reeve (i.e. steward) of a shire. Compare Reeve .
Muslim (mainly Nigeria, Sierra Leone, and Liberia): variant of   Sharif  . In Sierra Leone and Liberia this name is found mainly among the Mandinka people. Compare   Sirleaf  .

variants.name: Sirleaf
meanings: West African (Liberia): altered form of the predominantly Mandinka Muslim name   Sheriff  .

variants.name: Reeve
meanings: English (East Anglia): occupational name from Middle English reve, refe ‘reeve’ (Old English gerēfa). In medieval England a reeve was an official responsible for the administration of a manor, including organizing work done by the peasants on the land for their lord, collecting rents, selling produce, and so on.
Irish: variant of   Reeves   5.

variants.name: Shreve
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Sheriff  . Compare   Shreeve  ,   Shreves  .

variants.name: Sherriff
meanings: English: variant of   Sheriff  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Hampshire,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

(Yorkshire): habitational name from the southern English county so called, which derives its name from Hampton (i.e. the port of Southampton) + Old English scīr ‘division, district’. The County was formerly called Southamptonshire.

(Surrey and Kent): habitational name from the area of Hallamshire in southern Yorkshire, named from Hallam + Middle English schir ‘division’, ‘administrative region’ (Old English scīr). The surname is most common in Yorkshire, where this second derivation is most likely to be the source.

variants.name: Hallam
meanings: English (North Midlands): habitational name from Halam (Nottinghamshire) or from Kirk or West Hallam (Derbyshire), all named with the Old English dative plural halum ‘(at the) nooks or corners of land’ (from Old English halh ‘nook, recess’; see Hale 1). The name may also be derived from Hallam in Sheffield (Yorkshire), sometimes called Hallamshire, named from Old English hallum ‘(at the) rocks’, the dative plural of Old English hall ‘stone, rock’, Old Norse hallr.
English: possibly a variant of Allam with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Homsher
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of English   Hampshire  .

variants.name: Hamsher
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of   Hampshire  .

variants.name: Hanshaw
meanings: English: variant of   Hampshire  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Handschuh  .

nameMeanings.name: Burgess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name from Middle English burge(i)s, burgis, borgeis ‘inhabitant of a borough’ (strictly one possessing full municipal rights), ‘freeman of a borough’. Burgesses generally had tenure of land or buildings from a landlord by burgage. In medieval England burgage involved the payment of a fixed money rent (as opposed to payment in kind); in Scotland it involved payment in service, guarding the town. The -eis ending is from Latin -ensis (modern English -ese as in Portuguese). Compare Burger .
Americanized form of French   Bourgeois  .
History: Thomas Burgess came from England to MA c. 1630 and eventually settled in Sandwich, MA.

variants.name: Burger
meanings: German (also Bürger), Dutch, and English: status name for a freeman of a borough, especially one who was a member of its governing council. The term is a derivative of Middle High German burc, Middle Dutch burch, Middle English burg ‘(fortified) town’. The surname of German origin is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, Slovenia, and Croatia (see also 3 below). It also occurs as a Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname, but the reasons for its adoption are uncertain. The English name is found occasionally as a surname from the 13th century onward, but is not recorded as a vocabulary word until the 16th century. The usual English term was the Old French word burgeis ‘burgess’ (see Burgess ). This surname is also established in South Africa. Compare Buerger .
German: habitational name for someone from any of the many places called Burg.
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian Burgar, a status name of the same the same (Middle High) German origin as 1 above.

variants.name: Burges
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Burgess  .

variants.name: Burgis
meanings: English: variant of   Burgess  .

nameMeanings.name: for
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written ស, from a Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘white, fair’. Compare   Sar   1.
Cambodian: written សរ, from a Khmer word meaning ‘arrow’. Compare   Sar   2.
French: variant of   Saur  .

variants.name: Sar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Hak, Pang, Ry, Thay, Leang, Hai, Him, Chan, Heang, Sarin.  Cambodian Chamroeun, Savoeun, Sokhom, Vuthy.
Cambodian: written ស, from a Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘white, fair’. Compare   Sor   1.
Cambodian: written សរ, from a Khmer word meaning ‘arrow’. Compare   Sor   2.

nameMeanings.name: Hindon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Hindon (Wilts).

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon was born in 1480, in Tichborne, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Tichbourne III, Esq., was 20 and his mother, Margaret Martin, was 20. He married Anne White. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1535, in his hometown, at the age of 55, and was buried in Tichborne, Hampshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon est né en 1480, à Tichborne, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Tichbourne III, Esq., avait 20 ans et sa mère, Margaret Martin, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Anne White. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1535, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Tichborne, Hampshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon 1480 in Tichborne, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Tichbourne III, Esq. 20 und seine Mutter Margaret Martin 20. Er hat Anne White geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1535 im Alter von 55 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Tichborne, Hampshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon è nato il nel 1480 nel Tichborne, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Tichbourne III, Esq., aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Martin, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Anne White. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1535, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Tichborne, Hampshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon(이)가 1480년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 티ch본에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Tichbourne III, Esq.(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Martin(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Anne White(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1535년 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 함프실르 티ch본에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon nasceu em 1480, em Tichborne, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Tichbourne III, Esq., tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Martin, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne White. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1535, em sua cidade natal, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Tichborne, Hampshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon родился в 1480 г. в местности тичборн, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Tichbourne III, Esq. было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Martin, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne White. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1535 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности тичборн, хампшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas Tichborne, Sheriff of Hampshire, Burgess for Hindon nació en 1480, en Tichborne, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Tichbourne III, Esq., tenía 20 años y su mamá, Margaret Martin, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Anne White. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1535, en su pueblo, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Tichborne, Hampshire, Inglaterra.
